tool_input.summary:
Home Plate’s high-silica (often interpreted as opaline silica) is astrobiologically important not just because it requires water, but because it most plausibly records a *specific kind* of watery environment—hydrothermal/volcanic (e.g., hot-spring sinter or fumarolic/acid-sulfate alteration) rather than ordinary low-temperature weathering. That matters because:

- **Hydrothermal systems are high-priority habitats**: they provide long-lived heat, water, and steep chemical/redox gradients (energy sources) that can sustain microbial ecosystems even when the broader surface is harsh.
- **Silica is an exceptional biosignature preservative**: rapid silica precipitation can entomb cells/biofilms and preserve micro-textures and organics (as in terrestrial hot-spring sinters). Even if life was absent, the preservation potential makes silica deposits prime targets for biosignature searches.
- **It constrains aqueous chemistry and habitability**: very high silica implies intense leaching/precipitation pathways (hydrothermal or acid-sulfate), narrowing pH–temperature–water/rock conditions. Some scenarios can be harsh (acidic), but others can be habitable (neutral to mildly alkaline hot-spring settings), and either way they indicate *localized* aqueous niches.

Below are the most directly relevant PDS datasets for interrogating the Home Plate silica story using Spirit (MER-2) instrument observations (spectral ID, chemistry, textures, and context).

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Mini-TES emissivity spectra are the key Spirit dataset used to identify and map silica-rich (opaline) materials around Home Plate via diagnostic thermal-IR spectral shapes.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MTES-4-EMR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart of Spirit Mini-TES derived emissivity products; useful if you are working with legacy MER processing pipelines/literature products.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Calibrated Radiance Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated Mini-TES radiance is the upstream spectral product for independent/alternative emissivity retrievals and for checking data-quality assumptions that affect silica detections.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MTES-3-RDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated radiance (RDR) for Spirit Mini-TES, enabling legacy reprocessing/validation of silica identifications.

candidates.title: MER APXS Derived Oxide Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: APXS derived oxide chemistry (including SiO2 and major-element context) is central to quantifying how silica-rich the Home Plate materials are and distinguishing precipitation vs leaching scenarios.

candidates.title: MER1/
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 APXS derived oxide archive; often cited in older Home Plate compositional analyses and compatible with legacy workflows.

candidates.title: MER2 Microscopic Imager Science Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Microscopic Imager data let you examine fine-scale textures (e.g., coatings, nodular/sinter-like fabrics, vugs) relevant to formation mechanism and biosignature preservation potential.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MI-3-RDR-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated MI archive for Spirit; provides the same texture constraints in the legacy MER volume format.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Science Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Pancam context and multispectral imaging support geologic mapping of silica-rich units around Home Plate and help relate silica to stratigraphy/vents/alteration halos.

candidates.title: MER2-M-PANCAM-2-EDR-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Pancam science EDR volume for Spirit; useful for reproducing earlier Home Plate imaging products and mosaics cited in the literature.

candidates.title: MER MB Relative Iron Abundance Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Moessbauer-derived iron mineral constraints help test alteration pathways associated with silica (e.g., hydrothermal vs acid-sulfate) by characterizing Fe-bearing phases and oxidation state.

candidates.title: MER2-M-MB-4-SUMSPEC-SCI-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 Moessbauer summed spectra archive for Spirit; pairs with APXS/Mini-TES to build a coherent alteration model for the Home Plate silica-bearing materials.

candidates.title: MER Contact Science Target List Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Helps you locate which named targets/sols had contact-science measurements (APXS/MB/MI) on and around Home Plate, making it easier to reproduce the silica-related measurement context.

You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Why do the Home Plate silica deposits matter for astrobiology, beyond simply indicating water-rock interaction?
